front: Work: a fragmentary 'factless autobiography' credited to the bookkeeper Bernardo Soares, compiled from 500+ sheets found in a trunk
Work: translated by Richard Zenith and Margaret Jull Costa, who struggled to order its fragments
Work: the narrator's boss is named Vasques; he lives in a fourth-floor apartment and writes during rainy days
Work: opens with 'My soul is a hidden orchestra' in the chronological edition
Work: contains the observation that having read The Pickwick Papers is one of life's great tragedies, because one can't read it first again
Work: this author asserted 'to have touched the feet of Christ is no excuse for mistakes in punctuation' in this work
Author: Alberto Caeiro, the 'Master' heteronym, wrote The Keeper of Sheep on a 'triumphant day' and published a neopaganism program
Author: Alvaro de Campos, a Glasgow-educated naval engineer heteronym and Walt Whitman devotee, wrote 'The Tobacco Shop' and 'Opiario'
Author: Ricardo Reis, an Epicurean doctor heteronym, wrote odes to Lydia and was exiled to Brazil after a failed 1919 rebellion
Author: Jose Saramago wrote The Year of the Death of Ricardo Reis, in which that heteronym converses with this author's ghost in Lisbon
Author: lesser-known heteronyms include the Baron of Tieve (suicided from pure reason), astrologer Raphael Baldaya, and Englishman Alexander Search (died at twenty)
Work: a 44-poem collection structured around Portugal's coat of arms, containing the poem 'Ulysses' ('Myth is the nothing that is everything')
Work: contains O Mostrengo, about the spirit Adamastor who tells the protagonist 'my cape will be implacably hostile'
Author: best-known member of the Orpheu modernist movement; wrote for the magazine Orpheus
Author: as a teenager in Durban, South Africa, this author designed elaborate fake English-language newspapers
Author: Antonio Tabucchi wrote Requiem: A Hallucination featuring this author's spirit as 'the Guest'
Author: this author prophesied his posthumous legacy in the poem 'If I Die Young': 'roots may be hidden in the ground / But their flowers flower in the open air'
Author: wrote the poem 'Antinous' about 'the cold in Hadrian's soul'
